steps: Grow small thallus fragments for two weeks in petri dishes on Knop or BCD media at pH 5.7 and containing 0.7% (w/v) Gelzan (A in figure).
Transfer plants to Magenta pots on Knop medium or BCD medium (B and C in figure) at pH 5.7, containing 0.7% (w/v) Gelzan. Add 2mL of sterile water into the pot using a pipette.
Place pots in a Panasonic MLR-352 Versatile Environmental Test Chamber (or similar growth chamber / tissue cluture room) at 21 o C, 12 h of light and 12 h of dark, 1500 lux light intensity.
After approximately one month, antheridia start to appear. Add another 1-2 mL of sterile water into the pot using a pipette (D in figure).
After one more month sporophytes emerge (E in figure).
creator: Eftychis Frangedakis
